sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Precision Trading with Proshares Ultra Real Estate 2x Shares (URE) Risk Zones
feed.summary: This article analyzes Proshares Ultra Real Estate 2x Shares (URE), highlighting weak near-term sentiment and elevated downside risk. It provides specific institutional trading strategies across different risk profiles, including position trading, momentum breakout, and risk hedging, along with multi-timeframe signal analysis. The author, Rick O., presents specific entry and target zones for long and short positions, along with stop-loss levels.

feed.title: 2 Services Stocks with Solid Fundamentals and 1 That Underwhelm
feed.summary: This article analyzes three services stocks: Equifax (EFX), Automatic Data Processing (ADP), and Taboola (TBLA). While EFX is identified as a stock to sell due to declining operating margins and slow EPS growth, ADP and TBLA are highlighted as strong contenders with solid fundamentals, including impressive revenue growth, strong free cash flow, and increasing returns on capital, making them attractive for investors.

feed.title: T-Mobile CEO Says No MVNO Plans With Elon Musk's Starlink: 'It's Not Clear To Me...'
feed.summary: T-Mobile CEO Srini Gopalan has stated that there are no current plans for a Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) partnership with Elon Musk's Starlink. Gopalan indicated that T-Mobile considers MVNOs when there's an incremental addressable market, and it's unclear how a Starlink partnership would fit these criteria, despite an existing collaboration allowing T-Mobile customers to use Starlink's direct-to-cell technology for services like messaging and video calls. Starlink, which also faces competition from Amazon's Project Kuiper, had previously acquired spectrum licenses from EchoStar and has filed a trademark hinting at a possible future as a mobile carrier.

feed.title: MiniMed expects to close $644M IPO next week ahead of Medtronic split
feed.summary: MiniMed is set to close its $644 million IPO next week, with shares priced at $20 apiece, ahead of its planned separation from Medtronic. Medtronic will retain about 90.03% ownership initially. This IPO marks the first step in MiniMed becoming an independently traded company, utilizing proceeds for corporate purposes and debt repayment to Medtronic.

feed.title: Grandson of Reese’s Founder Takes on Hershey, Defending Family Recipe
feed.summary: Brad Reese, the 70-year-old grandson of the Reese's inventor, has become a vocal critic of Hershey, defending the original family recipe and brand legacy. He expresses concerns about ingredient changes and advocates for increased advertising and new product ventures for the Reese's brand. Hershey, however, states no alterations have been made to the popular peanut butter cups and that recipe tweaks are only for new shapes and sizes.

feed.title: What Can Trigger Costco Wholesale Stock’s Slide?
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale (COST) faces potential stock decline due to three main risks: peak membership saturation, margin erosion from expense creep and tariff uncertainty, and deteriorating accounts receivable management. The article points to slowing membership growth, increased SG&A, and rising accounts receivable as evidence of these threats. Historically, Costco's stock has seen significant dips during market downturns, and current financials show a high P/E ratio compared to sector medians.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific opens new distribution centre in Ireland
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has opened a new 70,000 sq.ft. distribution center in Bracetown, County Meath, Ireland, significantly expanding its in-country distribution capacity by over 400%. This investment strengthens the supply chain for Ireland's life sciences sector, enhancing access to critical materials and supporting drug discovery, clinical development, and manufacturing. The facility is designed with sustainability in mind, featuring an on-site solar system and operating on 100% renewable energy.

feed.title: Amcor expands Italian plant for recyclable film production
feed.summary: Amcor has launched a new production line at its Lugo di Vicenza site in Italy, focusing on the manufacture of recyclable, high-barrier films for various packaging needs. The expanded facility, covering nearly 7,000m², includes equipment for the entire production process, a new quality control laboratory, and an automated warehouse. This investment aims to meet the demand from sectors such as food, beverage, pet food, and healthcare, and to help companies comply with regulations requiring all packaging to be recyclable by 2030.

feed.title: William Blair Maintains Outperform for Stryker Corporation (SYK) March 2026
feed.summary: William Blair has reiterated an Outperform rating for Stryker Corporation (SYK) on March 5, 2026, citing international expansion and M&A as key drivers for potential upside. Despite the positive outlook, the firm did not provide a new price target, and the market reaction was muted with a slight share price dip. Meyka AI rates SYK with an "A" grade, indicating a constructive medium-term view for investors based on various performance metrics.

feed.title: Travelzoo Engages Attendees at ITB Berlin with Innovative Multi-Sensory Travel Display: What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Travelzoo (NASDAQ: TZOO) showcased an innovative multi-sensory travel display at ITB Berlin, the world's largest travel trade show, to engage attendees. The exhibit featured a 12-meter LED screen transporting visitors to global destinations with ultra-slow-motion visuals, complemented by curated fragrances, tactile elements, and healthy food. This immersive experience aimed to deepen emotional connections to travel and highlight Travelzoo's mission to build a community of passionate explorers by offering exclusive, vetted travel deals to over 30 million members.

feed.title: Coca-Cola expands AI use in marketing and product development
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is increasingly integrating AI into its marketing and product development processes, moving beyond experimental use to make it central to its operations. This strategic shift aims to drive "persuasion-led" growth by using AI for consumer behavior analysis, campaign adaptation, and even conceptualizing new products like Coca‑Cola Y3000 Zero Sugar. The company's expansion of AI reflects a broader industry trend of adopting AI for efficiency and improved targeting, especially as consumer spending patterns become more cautious.

feed.title: Agency Brief—Gartner warns on agency AI, Geico CMO signals no pitch
feed.summary: Gartner predicts that by 2029, half of proprietary agency AI platforms will become obsolete or sunset, highlighting a significant challenge for the advertising industry. This warning is part of an agency brief that also mentions Geico's CMO signaling no pitch, indicating stable agency relationships for the insurance giant. The article touches on broader themes of AI's impact on marketing and media transparency.

feed.title: Autodesk’s model for building an AI ‘moat’
feed.summary: Autodesk CEO Andrew Anagnost explains how his company is building an AI "moat" by focusing on high-stakes industries where precision is critical, complex contexts, and proprietary data combined with expert understanding. He notes that the company is shifting towards consumption-based and outcome-based pricing models to adapt to potential AI-driven headcount reductions among clients. Anagnost believes this strategy, combined with their long-term AI investments, will enable Autodesk and its customers to thrive in the evolving AI landscape.

feed.title: Home Depot Shares Sold by Berry Wealth Group LP
feed.summary: Berry Wealth Group LP significantly decreased its stake in The Home Depot, Inc. by selling 9,478 shares, reducing its total position to 2,866 shares. This move, reported in its latest 13F filing, means Home Depot now constitutes 3.8% of the firm's investment portfolio. While the sale suggests a potential shift in the investor's outlook, the exact rationale remains unclear.
